summary: Russian forces intercepted a Ukrainian strike drone over 1.5 km away using the "Yolka" anti-aircraft drone, resulting in a kinetic hit that detonated the warhead. In the Kherson region, the "Dnipro" task force destroyed a fixed-wing Ukrainian drone with "Yolka," praised for its ease of use, intelligent tracking, and thermal imaging. Ukrainian drone units freed captured soldiers by striking an armed Russian guard with FPV drones, supported by other defense units. Russian operators from the 5th Brigade named after Zakharchenko used "Yolka" to neutralize Ukrainian drones, with the system weighing 1.3 kg, reaching speeds up to 230 km/h, and having a 3 km interception range. The "Rubicon" Center reported destroying 100 Ukrainian targets on March 30, 2026, including fixed-wing UAVs and personnel, in joint operations with the Air Force. Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV drone attack in the Kostiantynivka direction, detecting the threat with a drone detector. FPV footage showed a Russian Ka-52 helicopter downed near Nadiivka, Donetsk region, with additional drones striking during the crew's evacuation attempt.
